name: SPARK.Containers.Functional.Sets

documentation.description:
Local package for renamings to avoid polluting the namespace in user
code.

simple_types.name: Iterable_Set
simple_types.code:
type Iterable_Set is private with
  Iterable =>
    (First       => First,
     Has_Element => Has_Element,
     Next        => Next,
     Element     => Element);

simple_types.name: Set
simple_types.code:
type Set is private with
  Default_Initial_Condition => Is_Empty (Set),
  Iterable                  => (First       => Iter_First,
                                Next        => Iter_Next,
                                Has_Element => Iter_Has_Element,
                                Element     => Iter_Element),
  Aggregate                 => (Empty       => Empty_Set,
                                Add_Unnamed => Aggr_Include),
  Annotate                  =>
    (GNATprove, Container_Aggregates, "Predefined_Sets");

documentation.description:
Sets are empty when default initialized.
"For in" quantification over sets should not be used.
"For of" quantification over sets iterates over elements.
Inclusion in a set works modulo equivalence, the whole equivalence class
is included/excluded at once. As equivalence classes might be infinite,
quantification over elements of a finite set could be infinite. Thus,
quantified expressions cannot be executed and should only be used in
disabled ghost code. This is enforced by having a special imported
procedure Check_Or_Fail that will lead to link-time errors otherwise.

subprograms.code: function "<=" (Left : Set; Right : Set) return Boolean

documentation.description: Set inclusion

subprograms.code: function "=" (Left : Set; Right : Set) return Boolean

documentation.description: Extensional equality over sets

subprograms.name: Add
subprograms.code: function Add (Container : Set; Item : Element_Type) return Set

documentation.description:
Return a new set containing all the elements of Container plus the
equivalence class of E.

subprograms.code: function Aggr_Eq_Elements (Left, Right : Element_Type) return Boolean

subprograms.code: function Choose (Container : Set) return Element_Type

documentation.description: Return an arbitrary element in Container

subprograms.name: Contains
subprograms.code: function Contains (Container : Set; Item : Element_Type) return Boolean

documentation.description: Return True if Item is contained in Container

subprograms.name: Copy_Element
subprograms.code: function Copy_Element (Item : Element_Type) return Element_Type

documentation.description:
Elements of containers are copied by numerous primitives in this
package. This function causes GNATprove to verify that such a copy is
valid (in particular, it does not break the ownership policy of SPARK,
i.e. it does not contain pointers that could be used to alias mutable
data).

subprograms.name: Element
subprograms.code: function Element (Iterator : Iterable_Set; Cursor : Set) return Element_Type

documentation.description:
The next element to be considered for the iteration is the result of
choose on Cursor.

documentation.description: Return a new empty set

documentation.description: In the first iteration, the cursor is the set associated with Iterator

documentation.description: Retrieve the set associated with an iterator

subprograms.name: Has_Element

documentation.description:
Return True on non-empty sets which can be reached by iterating over
Container.

subprograms.name: Included_Except

documentation.description:
Return True if Left contains only elements of Right except possibly
the equivalence class of Item.

subprograms.name: Included_In_Union

documentation.description: Return True if every element of Container is the union of Left and Right

subprograms.name: Includes_Intersection

documentation.description:
Return True if every element of the intersection of Left and Right is
in Container.

subprograms.code: function Intersection (Left : Set; Right : Set) return Set

documentation.description: Returns the intersection of Left and Right

documentation.description: A set is empty if it contains no element

subprograms.name: Is_Singleton

documentation.description: Return True Container only contains elements equivalent to New_Item

Iter_Next
Iterate

subprograms.name: Next
subprograms.code: function Next (Iterator : Iterable_Set; Cursor : Set) return Set

documentation.description:
At each iteration, remove the equivalence class of the considered

documentation.description: Return True if there are no equivalent elements in Left and Right

subprograms.name: Not_In_Both

documentation.description:
Return True if there are no elements in Container that are in Left and

subprograms.code: function Num_Overlaps (Left : Set; Right : Set) return Big_Natural

documentation.description: Number of equivalence classes that are both in Left and Right

subprograms.code: function Remove (Container : Set; Item : Element_Type) return Set

documentation.description:
Return a new set containing all the elements of Container except the

subprograms.name: Valid_Subset

documentation.description:
Return True on all sets which can be reached by iterating over
Container.

generic_instantiations.name: Eq_Elements_Checks
generic_instantiations.code:
package Eq_Elements_Checks is new
  SPARK.Containers.Parameter_Checks.Equivalence_Checks
    (T                   => Element_Type,
     Eq                  => Equivalent_Elements,
     Param_Eq_Reflexive  => Equivalent_Elements_Reflexive,
     Param_Eq_Symmetric  => Equivalent_Elements_Symmetric,
     Param_Eq_Transitive => Equivalent_Elements_Transitive);

documentation.description:
Check that the actual parameter for Equivalent_Elements is an
equivalence relation.

This constant should only be set to False when no particular handling
of equivalence over elements is needed, that is, Equivalent_Elements
defines an element uniquely.

formals.name: Equivalent_Elements

documentation.description:
Local package for renamings to avoid polluting the namespace in user
